name: Location_Scenario
lever_classification: governance — which city will host the primary office
consequences: Selecting this location scenario will determine the initial development costs, talent pool availability, and overall project timeline.
options: Establish a primary office in Los Angeles to leverage local industry connections
Choose Detroit as the central hub for its revitalized downtown area and diverse workforce
Prioritize Miami as the main base due to its favorable business climate and existing gaming infrastructure
review: The chosen location may not provide sufficient space for a growing team, potentially forcing relocation and disrupting momentum.

name: Partnership_Model
consequences: Selecting this partnership model will impact the project's financial stability, access to resources, and overall creative direction.
options: Strategic partnerships with major game publishers for exclusive distribution deals
Industry collaborations focusing on innovative technology integrations
Government grants aimed at promoting local economic growth through gaming
review: The chosen partnership model may not provide sufficient flexibility for future development or expansion, potentially limiting the project's long-term success.

name: Procedural_Generation
lever_classification: methodology — which data collection approach to use
consequences: Selecting this procedural generation approach will affect the game's overall scope, player engagement, and replayability.
options: Focus on generating realistic urban environments through advanced algorithms
Implement a dynamic weather system with real-time effects on gameplay
Develop a hybrid approach combining hand-crafted and procedurally generated content
review: The chosen procedural generation method may not account for the complexity of the game's narrative or character interactions, potentially leading to inconsistencies.

name: Heist_Mechanics
lever_classification: product — how to sequence the rollout phases
consequences: Selecting this heist mechanics approach will impact the game's replayability, player engagement, and overall difficulty curve.
options: Implement a traditional linear heist structure with set objectives
Develop a dynamic, non-linear heist system where players can choose their own approach
Introduce a hybrid model combining elements of both
review: The chosen heist mechanics may not provide sufficient variety or challenge for experienced players, potentially leading to burnout.

name: Morality_System
lever_classification: product — which data collection approach to use
consequences: Selecting this morality system will affect the game's overall narrative, player choices, and replayability.
options: Implement a binary choice system with clear moral implications
Develop a nuanced, context-dependent morality system where player choices have varying consequences
Introduce a dynamic morality system that adapts to the player's actions
review: The chosen morality system may not provide sufficient depth or complexity for players seeking a more immersive experience.

name: Vehicle_Customization
lever_classification: product — how to sequence the rollout phases
consequences: Selecting this vehicle customization approach will impact the game's overall feel, player engagement, and replayability.
options: Implement a limited customization system with fixed options
Develop an extensive vehicle customization system with real-time effects on gameplay
Introduce a hybrid model combining elements of both
review: The chosen vehicle customization method may not provide sufficient variety or depth for players seeking a more immersive experience.

name: Next_Gen_Graphics
lever_classification: product — which data collection approach to use
consequences: Selecting this next-gen graphics approach will affect the game's overall visual fidelity, player engagement, and system requirements.
options: Prioritize high-fidelity textures and detailed character models
Focus on advanced lighting and shadow techniques for a more immersive experience
Develop a hybrid approach combining elements of both
review: The chosen next-gen graphics method may not account for the complexity of the game's narrative or gameplay mechanics, potentially leading to performance issues.

name: Talent_Pool_Configuration
lever_classification: governance — how to structure the team
consequences: Attracting top-tier developers and artists will boost quality but also increase salaries, leading to higher operational costs.
options: Diversify talent by hiring freelancers for specific projects
Establish a full-time staff with competitive benefits and bonuses
Foster a collaborative culture through internships and mentorship programs
review: The options overlook the potential loss of institutional knowledge when relying on freelancers, while fostering a culture might lead to burnout if not managed properly.

name: Research_Partnerships
lever_classification: governance — how to collaborate with external partners
consequences: Partnering with research institutions will enhance game realism but also create intellectual property sharing complexities.
options: Collaborate with universities on data-driven crime studies
Join forces with think tanks to develop realistic economic models
Work with criminology experts to improve NPC behavior
review: The options assume a straightforward IP agreement, but the nuances of academic collaboration can lead to unexpected ownership disputes.

name: Developmental_Pace
lever_classification: methodology — how to structure the game's development
consequences: Accelerating development will speed up market entry but also increase the risk of bugs and quality issues.
options: Implement a phased release schedule for beta testing
Use agile methodologies to iterate quickly through development cycles
Maintain a traditional linear approach with rigorous quality control
review: The options neglect the potential impact on team morale when pushing too hard for speed, potentially leading to burnout and turnover.

name: Community_Relations
lever_classification: dissemination — how to engage with the gaming community
consequences: Engaging with fans through social media will enhance brand loyalty but also create expectations for updates and content.
options: Host regular community events and Q&A sessions
Develop a dedicated forum for discussion and feedback
Create a rewards program for loyal fans
review: The options overlook the potential for toxic behavior from some fans, which can harm the game's reputation if not managed properly.

name: IP_Protection_Strategies
lever_classification: governance — how to protect intellectual property
consequences: Implementing robust IP protection will safeguard intellectual property but also increase costs and administrative burdens.
options: Utilize standard industry contracts for licensing and distribution
Develop in-house IP management software
Outsource IP protection to specialized firms
review: The options assume a straightforward application of existing laws, but the evolving landscape of IP rights can lead to unexpected challenges.

name: Marketing_Campaign_Strategy
lever_classification: dissemination — how to promote the game
consequences: Running a targeted marketing campaign will boost visibility but also increase costs and potentially alienate certain demographics.
options: Develop a social media-focused campaign targeting the core gamer audience
Create a series of influencer partnerships to reach broader audiences
Invest in traditional advertising channels such as TV and print
review: The options neglect the potential for oversaturation if not carefully managed, potentially leading to decreased interest over time.

name: Supply_Chain_Management
lever_classification: operations — how to manage the game's physical infrastructure
consequences: Optimizing supply chain logistics will reduce costs but also increase reliance on third-party vendors.
options: Establish a network of local suppliers for hardware and materials
Outsource procurement to specialized firms
Develop in-house manufacturing capabilities
review: The options assume a stable market, but supply chain disruptions can have significant impacts on production timelines and costs.

name: Studio_Partnerships
lever_classification: governance — who oversees the review process
consequences: Forming strategic collaborations with top game development studios brings in fresh talent and ideas, but may compromise creative control.
options: Partner with a renowned AAA studio for resource sharing
Join forces with an indie developer to bring innovative ideas
Maintain full ownership through an internal team
review: Securing partnerships might overlook the long-term implications of IP ownership and potential studio politics, potentially hamstringing future game development.

name: Innovation_Grant_Program
lever_classification: governance — who oversees the review process
consequences: Applying for government innovation grants provides access to significant funding but comes with strict guidelines and potential bureaucratic delays.
options: Focus on securing a large, one-time grant for upfront costs
Apply for smaller, recurring grants to maintain flexibility
Develop an in-house program to foster innovation without external dependency
review: Grant applications might overlook the long-term implications of funding constraints and potential strings attached, potentially limiting future development.

name: Community_Involvement
lever_classification: dissemination — how to engage with the target audience
consequences: Engaging with the gaming community through events and social media enhances game visibility but requires significant resources and potential controversy.
options: Host large-scale, public gameplay events for buzz generation
Foster a strong online presence through influencer partnerships and contests
Maintain a low-key approach, focusing on internal testing and feedback
review: Community outreach might overlook the potential risks of negative publicity and community backlash, potentially damaging the game's reputation.

name: Developmental_Pipeline
lever_classification: operations — how development tasks are managed
consequences: Establishing a robust development pipeline ensures consistent game releases but may compromise on quality and innovation.
options: Follow a traditional, annual release schedule for stability
Adopt an iterative approach, focusing on continuous improvement
Develop a flexible pipeline that adapts to changing market conditions
review: Prioritizing a developmental pipeline might overlook the potential risks of burnout and decreased creativity among developers, potentially leading to subpar game quality.
